summary: Adapts refresh.Fetcher to the Source interface for Telegram channels, translating integer message IDs to string checkpoints

requires: Checkpoint is a decimal integer string representing the highest Telegram message ID seen (or empty for initial fetch)
guarantees: Invalid (non-numeric) checkpoint is silently treated as 0 — no error returned
Checkpoint is preserved unchanged when no new messages are returned
New checkpoint is the highest message ID string across all returned messages

state: Stateless; delegates all state to the injected Fetcher

observations.text: This is the glue between the source system (internal/source) and the refresh system (internal/refresh). It depends on refresh.Fetcher, which is a boundary package for this scope. The source/telegram adapter is distinct from internal/telegram — the former is a source abstraction, the latter is the actual Telegram MTProto client.

Title and URL fields of SourceMessage are always empty — Telegram messages have no title or external URL in the FetchedMessage struct. This differentiates Telegram messages from RSS/HN/Reddit messages in downstream processing.
Checkpoint computation scans all messages for the max TelegramMsgID, starting from fetched[0].TelegramMsgID as the initial max. This assumes the fetcher may return messages out of ID order.

Silent treatment of invalid checkpoint ('not-a-number' → 0) means a corrupted checkpoint causes a full re-fetch from the beginning rather than an error. This is confirmed by the test TestTelegramSource_FetchMessages_InvalidCheckpoint.
